verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 (Cysteine-Rich Secretory Protein 1) is a secreted protein primarily found in the male reproductive tract (epididymis). It is secreted into the epididymal lumen and binds to the surface of sperm during epididymal transit, associating with the sperm plasma membrane (particularly the acrosomal and tail regions) in a wash-resistant manner that persists through capacitation. This stable post-translational attachment to the sperm cell surface makes it contextual: it reaches the outer face of the PM, but only in the specific context of sperm/epididymal cells, not as a ubiquitous surface protein.
